findings.title: Mutual recursion: 5 functions in call cycle (complexity 10)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 5 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/arguments.c::args_free:426 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/arguments.c::args_free_value:399 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/arguments.c::args_free_values:416 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/cmd.c::cmd_free:546 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/cmd.c::cmd_list_free:630. Aggregate complexity: 10.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/server.c::server_loop:263` dominates 51 functions (3%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/server.c::server_loop:263` dominates 51 of 1818 functions (2.8%). PageRank percentile: 0%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/cmd-queue.c::cmdq_next:729, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/cmd-wait-for.c::cmd_wait_for_flush:243, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/control.c::control_discard:812, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/format-draw.c::format_draw:688, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tmux/format-draw.c::format_width:1082 (+57 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Architectural Bottleneck: mode_tree_draw (architectural hub)
findings.description:
Function 'mode_tree_draw' is called by 17 functions and has complexity 46. Changes here are high-risk.

Betweenness centrality: 0.6561 - this function sits on many execution paths between other functions.

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 46 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=15, p95=23, mean=7.3, n=1818
findings.suggested_fix: This is a critical hub. Ensure comprehensive test coverage, add defensive error handling, and consider circuit breaker pattern.
findings.why_it_matters: Bottlenecks are single points of failure that amplify bugs. High fan-in with high complexity means many callers depend on risky code.

findings.title: Long method: server_client_check_mouse (1599 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'server_client_check_mouse' has 1599 lines (effective threshold: 200).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 10 functions (reduced severity)
High complexity density: 0.45 (complexity 718 / 1599 lines)
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Long method: style_parse (203 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'style_parse' has 203 lines (effective threshold: 200).

**Graph Analysis:**
High complexity density: 0.52 (complexity 105 / 203 lines)
findings.suggested_fix:
High complexity density - this function does too much logic.
1. Extract conditional branches into helper functions
2. Use early returns to reduce nesting
3. Consider the Strategy pattern for varying behaviors
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Architectural Bottleneck: tty_cursor (utility function - expected high fan-in)
findings.description:
Function 'tty_cursor' is called by 15 functions and has complexity 67. Changes here are high-risk.

Betweenness centrality: 0.0257 - this function sits on many execution paths between other functions.

**Note:** This function was identified as a utility. High fan-in is expected, but the high complexity may still warrant attention.

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 67 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=15, p95=23, mean=7.3, n=1818
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this utility into smaller, focused helpers. High complexity in shared utilities is risky.
findings.why_it_matters: Bottlenecks are single points of failure that amplify bugs. High fan-in with high complexity means many callers depend on risky code.
